title: thelypteris pygmaea
intro: Thelypteris pygmaea, also known as the dwarf maidenhair fern, is a small species of fern that belongs to the family Thelypteridaceae. The leaves of this plant have been traditionally used in folk medicine for their medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antibacterial effects. Thelypteris pygmaea has been used to treat various health issues, such as respiratory problems, digestive issues, and skin conditions, due to its health benefits in reducing inflammation and improving immune function. Herbal preparations of this plant include infusions, decoctions, and salves, which can be used topically or internally to harness its medicinal properties.
